takeaways: Janus Living's upsized $840M IPO opened today on NYSE under 'JAN' — the first pure-play senior housing REIT to go public in years, signaling renewed Wall Street conviction in the sector.
CRE capital is flowing: conventional mortgage rates at 4.99–5.19%, Colliers forecasts 15–20% growth in 2026 sales activity, and lending conditions are at their most constructive since 2021.
AI proptech investment surged to $16.7B in 2025 (+68%), but agentic AI advances are disrupting platforms before they scale — and 44% of CRE investment committees still distrust AI-generated analysis.

items.headline: Janus Living Prices Upsized $840M IPO — Pure-Play Senior Housing REIT Begins Trading on NYSE as "JAN"
items.summary: Healthpeak's spinoff Janus Living priced its upsized IPO at $20/share, raising $840M above its $740M target. With 34 communities (10,422 units) across 10 states, it is the only US publicly traded REIT focused exclusively on senior housing under RIDEA structures. Healthpeak retains ~83.6% post-IPO.

items.headline: JLL: Senior Housing Transactions Hit Decade-High $24B — 86% of Investors Want More Exposure
items.summary: JLL's rolling four-quarter senior housing transaction volume reached $24B at year-end 2025, the strongest pace in over a decade. 86% of surveyed investors plan to increase seniors housing exposure in 2026, up 10 points YoY, driven by tight new supply and accelerating demographic demand.

items.headline: National Healthcare Properties Acquires 13 AL Communities for $64M in JV with Discovery Senior Living
items.summary: National Healthcare Properties announced a $64M joint venture with Discovery Senior Living to acquire 13 assisted living communities (592 units, 8 states). The deal reflects continued consolidation in the AL segment as operators and capital partners team up to capture rising demand.

items.headline: Fortress Launches 1031 Exchange Platform with 40% Senior Housing Allocation, Targeting $100–200M
items.summary: Fortress Investment Group launched Fortress Real Estate Exchange, a 1031 platform targeting $100–200M in 2026, with ~40% allocated to senior living. The structure lets investors defer capital gains by rolling proceeds into diversified CRE assets including senior housing.

items.headline: CRE Rate Update: Conventional Mortgages at 4.99–5.19%; SOFR at 3.67–3.71%; Fed Holds at 3.50–3.75%
items.summary: As of March 22, conventional commercial mortgage rates opened at 4.99–5.19% and SOFR is tracking at ~3.67–3.71%. The Fed held at 3.50–3.75% on March 18 with one dissent favoring a cut. Colliers forecasts 15–20% growth in CRE sales activity for 2026 as institutions and cross-border capital return to market.

items.headline: 2026 CRE Lending Most Constructive Since 2021 — All Capital Sources Active, Ample Dry Powder
items.summary: CBRE and CRE Daily report 2026 is the most constructive lending environment since 2021: all debt capital sources remain active, lender participation is strengthening, and borrowing costs are stabilizing. Institutional and cross-border investors are returning with sizable dry powder targeting yield-generating assets.

items.headline: 44% of CRE Investment Committees Distrust AI Analysis — Data Security Risks Add New Pressure
items.summary: Commercial Observer reports 44% of investment committees distrust AI-generated analysis and only 27% are confident in AI for financial underwriting. The bottleneck is governance and explainability, not capability. Simultaneously, AI data integration is opening new security vulnerabilities at CRE firms.

items.headline: Proptech VC Surges to $16.7B in 2025 (+68%) — Rapid AI Advances Now Disrupting the Stack
items.summary: Proptech investment hit $16.7B in 2025 (+68% YoY), with AI-native platforms capturing ~$4.5B. Bisnow reports VCs are regrouping as rapid AI advances unsettle the sector — platforms built on 2024-era models face disruption from next-generation agentic alternatives before achieving meaningful scale.

items.headline: Agentic AI Reshapes CRE Operations — From Content Generation to Autonomous Execution
items.summary: ICSC confirms agentic AI is 2026's defining CRE tech shift: moving beyond content to autonomous decision-making and workflow execution. Operators deploy AI agents for procurement, pricing, and leasing. Practical AI now surfaces portfolio revenue opportunities and automates routine tasks end-to-end.

items.headline: McKinsey 2026: CEO AI Ownership Doubles — Half Say Their Job Is on the Line If AI Doesn't Deliver
items.summary: McKinsey's State of Organizations 2026 finds CEO ownership of AI strategy has doubled YoY, with ~75% of CEOs now serving as primary AI decision-makers. Half believe their positions are at risk if AI investments don't materialize. Companies plan to double AI spending from 0.8% to ~1.7% of revenues.

items.headline: DDI 2026: 71% of Leaders Under Elevated Stress — Bench Confidence and Retention Hit New Lows
items.summary: DDI's 2026 Leadership Trends report finds 71% of leaders are under increased stress and 40% are considering leaving. 77% of CHROs lack confidence in bench strength for critical roles. US layoffs hit a 10-year high in early 2026, creating simultaneous talent pressure and leadership pipeline fragility.

items.headline: Neurowellness Tops 2026 Wellness Trends — Precision Nervous System Optimization Goes Mainstream
items.summary: The Global Wellness Summit named neurowellness the #1 wellness trend for 2026 — using tech to objectively measure and retrain stress and resilience in real time. A consumer backlash against over-optimization is simultaneously fueling demand for human-centered, tech-free social wellness experiences.
